reason: Both answers correctly reposition around forecasting as a narrow, ownable category, drop "AI-powered" as table stakes, note the buyer shift to ops/COO, and demote PM to supporting proof — hitting all the key points. A is crisp and has a nice diagnostic test ("Monday with AI" vs "who's free in 3 weeks"). B matches that but adds two things of real value A lacks: a sharper economic framing tied to concrete dollar impact, and a caveat to verify the forecasting is actually superior to entrenched niche players (Float/Runn) before betting the brand on it — an important risk check given those competitors already own this exact wedge. That risk-awareness makes B marginally more rigorous without adding padding.
